events.headline: Tornado Warning issued for Fairfield County until 4:15 PM EDT
events.summary: The National Weather Service in New York has issued a Tornado Warning for Fairfield County in southwestern Connecticut until 4:15 PM EDT. A severe thunderstorm capable of producing a tornado was located near Bridgeport, moving northeast at 35 mph. TAKE COVER NOW. Move to a basement or an interior room on the lowest floor of a sturdy building. Avoid windows.

events.headline: Large-scale power outage affecting 45,000 customers in Henrico County
events.summary: Dominion Energy has reported a major transmission failure affecting approximately 45,000 customers across Henrico County and portions of Chesterfield County. Crews are on site. Estimated restoration time is unknown at this time. Affected areas include Short Pump, Innsbrook, and West End neighborhoods. Customers with medical equipment should seek alternate power sources immediately.

events.headline: Tropical Storm Watch in effect for Nassau County through Saturday evening
events.summary: A Tropical Storm Watch has been issued for Nassau County as Tropical Storm Debby continues to move northward along the Atlantic coast. Tropical storm conditions — sustained winds of 39 mph or greater — are possible within the watch area beginning Friday evening. Residents should prepare now. Have an emergency kit ready, know your evacuation zone, and monitor official sources for updates.

events.headline: Large protest march expected on National Mall Saturday — police presence increased
events.summary: DC Metropolitan Police have announced traffic and road restrictions around the National Mall for Saturday beginning at 8 AM following permit applications for a large demonstration expected to draw 10,000 to 50,000 participants. Metro is advising riders to expect delays on the Blue, Orange, and Silver lines. Several roads including Constitution Avenue will be closed to vehicle traffic.

events.headline: I-95 northbound closed at Exit 22 following multi-vehicle accident — delays expected
events.summary: PennDOT is reporting a full closure of I-95 northbound at Exit 22 (Girard Avenue) following a multi-vehicle accident involving a tractor-trailer. Emergency responders are on scene. Motorists are advised to use I-676 as an alternate route. The closure is expected to remain in effect for at least 3 hours. No fatalities have been reported at this time.

events.headline: Severe Thunderstorm Watch for New York City metro area until 10 PM EDT
events.summary: The Storm Prediction Center has issued a Severe Thunderstorm Watch for the New York City metro area until 10 PM EDT tonight. Conditions are favorable for the development of severe thunderstorms with large hail up to 1.5 inches in diameter, wind gusts up to 70 mph, and heavy rainfall. A few tornadoes cannot be ruled out.

events.headline: Heat Advisory in effect through Friday evening for Suffolk and Middlesex Counties
events.summary: The National Weather Service has issued a Heat Advisory for Suffolk and Middlesex Counties through Friday at 8 PM EDT. Heat index values up to 103 degrees are expected. The combination of hot temperatures and high humidity will create a dangerous situation. Drink plenty of fluids, stay in an air-conditioned room, and check in on relatives and neighbors.

events.headline: Dense Fog Advisory until 10 AM EDT for Albany and Dutchess Counties
events.summary: The National Weather Service has issued a Dense Fog Advisory until 10 AM EDT for Albany and Dutchess Counties. Visibility will be one quarter mile or less at times. If driving, slow down, use your headlights, and leave plenty of distance ahead of you.

events.headline: Coastal Flood Statement for the Chesapeake Bay tidal areas through Thursday
events.summary: Minor coastal flooding is possible during times of high tide through Thursday evening for low-lying areas along the Chesapeake Bay tidal rivers. Up to one foot of inundation is possible in vulnerable areas. Residents in flood-prone locations should monitor water levels and be prepared to move vehicles and valuables to higher ground.

events.headline: Air Quality Alert — Code Orange for NJ and Philadelphia region through Thursday
events.summary: An Air Quality Alert has been issued for Code Orange — Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups — for the New Jersey and Philadelphia area through Thursday evening. Ground-level ozone concentrations are forecast to reach Code Orange levels. Children, the elderly, and those with respiratory conditions should limit prolonged or heavy outdoor exertion.
